name: Rakibul Islam Rayhan
heroStatement: I build end-to-end web experiences — from database schemas to polished interfaces.

about.intro: I'm a developer who started with pure curiosity and basic web tinkering. What began as tweaking HTML templates evolved into building complete applications from scratch.
about.journey: I started with frontend — React, Tailwind, JavaScript — and got serious about it by completing Programming Hero's web development course. That gave me structure: proper component architecture, state management, API integration. I also took a UI/UX course at Shikhbe Shobai, which changed how I think about interfaces — now I design before I code.
about.currentFocus: Recently, I've been pushing into full-stack territory. I'm working with PostgreSQL, Prisma ORM, and cloud databases like NeonDB and Supabase. I want to own projects end-to-end — from the database schema to the deployment pipeline. TypeScript has become essential to my workflow.
about.workStyle: I enjoy turning complex requirements into clean, intuitive interfaces. There's something satisfying about shipping a feature that just works — no friction, no confusion. I care about the details: loading states, error handling, responsive layouts that don't break on edge cases.
about.hobbies: When I'm not coding, I write tutorials and documentation — explaining things forces me to actually understand them. I listen to a lot of tech and design podcasts during commutes. It keeps me connected to the broader industry conversation and exposes me to problems I haven't encountered yet.
